 Suitable for coastal passages or navigation through areas of high traffic. This mode 
focuses on close proximity awareness for nearshore navigation. Acoustic alarms will 
be triggered only by dangerous targets.

 Suitable for offshore passages. Alarm sensitivity will be higher than for coastal 
mode. 
 Zoom and Pan: 
o In both color and thermal view, you are able to pass from the full field of view to a 
narrow-focused view by selecting  
o You can then pan left, right and come back to the middle by   
 Clicking on the arrows  
 Navigating the cursor on the horizon bar

o You may come back to the wide vision by selecting

 The current software version installed on your Watchkeeper 
 The storage space available on your unit. (For AI feedback recording).

Using the Interface: 
 Views: Thermal (only for Watchkeeper 320, 640 and 1280) or Color. 
o To access the thermal view, select   
o The thermal view shows the live images from the thermal camera with the following field 
of view (Horizontal x Vertical): 
 Watchkeeper 320: 24°x19° 
 Watchkeeper 640: 24°x19° 
 Watchkeeper 1280: 48°x19° 
o The thermal view works day and night and is particularly adequate for night or low 
visibility conditions. 
o To access the color view, select  
o The Color view shows the live images from the low-light camera with a field of view of 
130ºx65º.  
o It works in daylight but also in low-light conditions like dawn or dusk.

 Each view will display bounding boxes around the detections.

 Navigation modes: 
o  
 Suitable to use when entering and leaving harbours, marinas or crowded bays. The 
different views will show and highlight the objects detected, but all the alarms are

excerpts.query: operation daily use display menu controls operator
excerpts.text:
User Guide 
Watchkeeper series 
Doc SEAAI-442870296-11 
Revision:  
26 November 2025 
Page 11 of 18

The information contained in this document is proprietary to SEA.AI GmbH and is to be used only for the purpose for which it is supplied.

o The Modes tab explains the differences between the 3 Navigation modes.

o The Personalization tab will allow you to: 
 Choose the interface theme on your display: Light for daylight, Dark for low-light 
conditions, and Low contrast for night navigation to preserve your night vision. 
 Choose the display scale to have different icon sizes and adapt best to your display 
size. 
 Choose the thermal polarity in your thermal view (White is Hot: Normal or Black is 
Hot: Inverted) 
 Enable or disable the voice alarm that will alert when an object is detected, based 
on its threat level and direction. 
 Enable or disable the NMEA 2000 buzzer to play sound alarms on external Buzzers. 
 Choose the distance unit used to display. You can choose between meters and feet. 
Over 1 nautical mile the distance will be shown in Nautical miles.

o The Setup tab will allow you to: 
 Select and configure the NMEA Gateway model to be used.

Revision:  
26 November 2025 
Page 8 of 18

The information contained in this document is proprietary to SEA.AI GmbH and is to be used only for the purpose for which it is supplied.

Connecting Onboard PC: 
o  Download the Sea.AI Watchkeeper Windows/Mac application from Sea.AI Document library 
o Open the application. Your PC will find automatically the Watchkeeper if it’s connected to 
the same network.

Connecting Onboard MFD: 
o The computing power of your MFD might be limiting our ability to display the best version 
of our application, therefore we adapt to give you the best out of it. Depending on your MFD 
brand and model, our connection protocol and user interface will differ to ensure the best 
performance.  
o Please refer to our MFD compatibility page on our website:  
https://www.sea.ai/product-watchkeeper/mfd-compatibility/ 
o To start the SEA.AI App on your MFD – go to 3rd party Apps and tap on the SEA.AI Icon. The 
Icon will appear automatically once SEA.AI is connected and turned on. Start-up of the 
system and discovery in the MFD network can take several minutes.

6. Alarms

 AI DetecƟon: The object classification includes 6 different classes: 
Ship  
Motorboat  
Sailing boat  
Buoy  
Person in the water  
Unknown Hazard

How the AI decides what to show 
Our AI looks at every object it detects and calculates how conﬁdent it is about the object’s type. If 
the conﬁdence is high enough (the required level varies depending on the type of object), the 
system will display that class. If the conﬁdence doesn’t reach the required level, the object will 
simply appear as Unknown Hazard, so you’re sƟll aware that something is there, even if it can’t be 
clearly idenƟﬁed.

 Smart Alarms: Our smart alarm prioritizes risks and provides clear warnings on the potential 
hazards you may face. It is based on the distance and bearing of the detection. A frontal detection 
will have a higher level of threat than a side one. 
o Alarm tags: 
 Threat levels: Object (Blue), Warning (Yellow), Danger (Red)

Page 17 of 18

The information contained in this document is proprietary to SEA.AI GmbH and is to be used only for the purpose for which it is supplied.

7. Maintenance and Troubleshooting 
Regular Maintenance: 
 Clean camera lenses weekly using approved solutions. 
 Check cables integrity monthly. 
 Update firmware via USB or network connection quarterly. 
Troubleshooting Tips: 
 No Video Feed: Check camera power and cable connections and reboot your system. 
 False Alarms: Adjust detection sensitivity by changing navigation mode. 
 System Lag: Reboot and verify system resources. 
 If you encounter many false detections, please use the AI feedback and help us get better.

feedback tool  
 AI Feedback procedure: 
- Record raw images by clicking on the AI feedback tool . 
- Select the type of feedback that you want to share with Sea.AI. 
- The recording is on, you can stop it when you want, and it will be saved

8. Warranty and Support

SEA.AI provides a 2-year limited warranty covering manufacturing defects (See our Terms and conditions 
at www.sea.ai/termsconditions). 
Activate your product and warranty by registering at: https://connect.sea.ai/account/register

For support: 
 You can have access to our Frequently Asked Questions through this address: https://sea.ai/faq 
 Contact your dealer or installer for any support. 
 Email service@sea.ai for direct support from our Professional Services team.

4. Operating Instructions 
Product registration and activation: 
To ensure your new SEA.AI Watchkeeper delivers optimal performance and stays up to date with the latest 
advancements in Artificial Intelligence, periodic updates are essential. 
To receive Software Updates and your product key to activate your SEA.AI Watchkeeper you need to 
register your product.  
 Register your product easily by logging into your SEA.AI Connect account at: 
https://connect.sea.ai/account/login 
 If you do not have an account yet, you can simply create one at: 
https://connect.sea.ai/account/register 
Powering On: 
 Ensure all components are connected (see connection diagram in Installation guide). 
 Power on the system via the main control panel. 
Connecting External Devices: 
 How to connect your mobile devices (phone, tablet)? 
o Download the latest version of the SEA.:AI App. 
App Store:   
Google Play Store:

The SEA.AI System is intended to provide additional assistance to the User and does not exempt the User 
from strict compliance with the usual rules and practices of navigation safety and international maritime 
regulations. Users must be vigilant at all times and ensure that they have the required knowledge in terms 
of navigation. They must not rely on the use of the SEA.AI System to make navigation choices. Ensure the 
system is installed by certified professionals. Do not attempt to open or modify internal components.

3. Installation 
Note: Installation must be completed by a certified SEA.AI installer. Please check the installation guide in 
the SEA.AI document library. Accessible under www.sea.ai/document-library/ in the section Watchkeeper. 
Positioning Tips: 
 Mount the camera in a high location with a clear, unobstructed field of view. 
 Adjust the tilt angle by rotating the camera to compensate for boat trim at cruising speed. 
 Use the thermal camera as the primary reference during positioning.

Watchkeeper looking too high

Watchkeeper looking straight ahead. Horizon must be visible at cruising speed. 
(It is recommended to have 20% to 30% of the thermal image above the horizon)

5. Update your Watchkeeper 
Update with your computer: 
 Use your computer to download the latest Sea.AI package for your Watchkeeper on connect.sea.ai 
https://connect.sea.ai/account/login 
 Once the package is downloaded on your computer, access your Watchkeeper app and upload it 
into the settings/system update section. 
 If you have any problem with this procedure, please contact your local dealer/installer.

Update with your smartphone: 
 You can also update from your smartphone. Make sure your SEA.AI application on your smartphone 
(Apple App Store or Google Play Store) is up to date. 
At your first connection, make sure you enable notifications for new software releases:
